top_stories.title: Microsoft Makes Passkeys Default: What Identity And Security Leaders Need To Do
top_stories.summary: Microsoft announced that passkeys will become the default authentication method for all users in Microsoft Entra ID starting September 1, 2026. The change will automatically enable passkey authentication, marking a significant shift in phishing-resistant MFA adoption across the Microsoft identity platform.

top_stories.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
top_stories.summary: Identity-based attacks have surpassed exploits as the leading root cause of ransomware incidents. MFA was present in 97% of credential-based attacks yet failed to prevent compromise, highlighting critical gaps in authentication defenses.

top_stories.title: Claude can now sign into websites with 1Password without exposing your credentials
top_stories.summary: 1Password has launched a beta integration called "1Password for Claude" that allows Anthropic's Claude AI assistant to authenticate into websites during browser tasks without exposing user credentials or secrets. The integration works across individual, family, and business 1Password plans and requires Claude Desktop on macOS, the 1Password desktop app, and the 1Password browser extension.

top_stories.title: Oak raised $60M to give every user, machine, and AI agent one identity system
top_stories.summary: Oak, an Israeli identity startup, emerged from stealth with a $60M seed round to build a unified identity system covering human users, machines, and AI agents. The company is positioning itself in the identity governance and access management space, targeting the growing challenge of managing non-human identities alongside traditional user identities.

top_stories.title: n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
top_stories.summary: A token exchange vulnerability in n8n's workflow automation platform allowed attackers to authenticate as other users by exploiting improper JWT validation on Enterprise instances with multiple external token issuers. The flaw caused the system to match incoming JWTs on the `sub` claim alone while ignoring the `iss` (issuer) claim, enabling cross-issuer account takeover without requiring the victim's credentials.

news.title: Week in review: High severity WordPress vulnerabilities, fake OAuth IDs bypass sign-in logs
news.summary: A weekly security roundup covers a technique where fake OAuth client IDs are used to bypass sign-in logs, along with WordPress vulnerabilities and other security news. The OAuth bypass item is directly relevant to IAM professionals as it involves identity federation and authentication audit trail evasion.

news.title: Microsoft warns of surge in ACR Stealer attacks on customers
news.summary: Microsoft has observed a surge in ACR Stealer malware attacks targeting enterprise customers, with the malware focused on stealing browser-stored passwords and authentication tokens. The campaign directly targets credential and session token theft, undermining browser-based authentication mechanisms used across enterprise environments.

news.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
news.summary: Okta's Red Team discovered and reported a denial-of-service vulnerability in OpenSSL, dubbed "HollowByte," which allows an 11-byte TLS request to cause an unpatched server to allocate up to 131 KB of memory that is never released until the process restarts. OpenSSL quietly shipped a fix in June without a CVE, advisory, or changelog entry, prompting Okta to publish its own disclosure.

news.title: OpenSSL Fixes HollowByte Memory Exhaustion Bug
news.summary: Okta's Red Team discovered and disclosed a denial-of-service vulnerability in OpenSSL, named HollowByte, involving an 11-byte flaw that allows remote attackers to exhaust server memory. The vulnerability has been patched by OpenSSL following Okta's responsible disclosure.

news.title: ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
news.summary: ACR Stealer, an infostealer active since 2024, is being used to steal browser passwords, session tokens, and Microsoft 365 documents including files from OneDrive and SharePoint. The malware is delivered via ClickFix lures that trick users into manually executing malicious commands, with Microsoft's Defender Experts team documenting two delivery chains.

news.title: Kuehn Law Encourages Investors of SailPoint, Inc. to Contact Law Firm
news.summary: Kuehn Law, PLLC is investigating whether SailPoint, Inc. officers and directors breached their fiduciary duties to shareholders. The shareholder litigation firm is encouraging SailPoint investors to contact them regarding the investigation.

news.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider hacking group were sentenced to 5.5 years each for the 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London that compromised 148 systems. The attack forced all 27,000 TfL employees to reset their passwords in person, highlighting the scale of credential compromise involved.

news.title: Zoom Patches Critical Windows Flaw That Could Enable Account Takeover
news.summary: Zoom has patched a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-53412, CVSS 9.8) in Zoom Workplace for Windows that could enable account takeover. The flaw, caused by improper input validation, affects versions before 7.0.0 and multiple branches of the Zoom Workplace VDI Client for Windows.

news.title: Building Secure APIs with OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect
news.summary: An educational resource covers API security implementation using OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect, addressing authentication and authorization flows. The content includes token management, PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange), and best practices for securing APIs.

news.title: ESDS Launches Swaraj Nandi and Swaraj Hansa, Two Sovereign Enterprise Security Platforms Built Entirely in India - The Tribune
news.summary: ESDS launched two enterprise security platforms, Swaraj Nandi (focused on privileged access management) and Swaraj Hansa (security operations/alerting), built entirely in India as sovereign solutions. The platforms target privileged access governance and security monitoring, positioning as domestic alternatives for Indian enterprises.

news.title: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
news.summary: Microsoft published guidance on applying least privilege principles to AI agents, covering identity, access controls, and tool binding. The article addresses how autonomous AI agents require strong identity verification, scoped permissions, and auditing controls to remain secure.

news.title: Keeper Security Closes the Standing Privilege Gap in Cloud Identity with Keeper Privileged Cloud
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched Keeper Privileged Cloud, a new just-in-time access capability within its KeeperPAM platform. The feature grants privileged credentials only when needed and automatically revokes them when a session expires, addressing standing privilege risks in cloud identity environments.

news.title: Microsoft introduces registry policy to automate Windows SSO consent
news.summary: Microsoft has introduced a registry-based control allowing IT administrators to automatically bypass SSO permission prompts on managed Windows 11 devices. The feature targets enterprise environments running specific Windows 11 versions, enabling automated consent management for single sign-on workflows.

news.title: Microsoft makes Windows SSO prompts easier to manage
news.summary: Microsoft is introducing a registry-based policy allowing IT administrators to automatically accept Windows SSO permission prompts on Windows 11 versions 24H2 and 25H2 devices managed through Microsoft Entra ID. The policy applies specifically to Entra ID-managed devices, excluding personal Microsoft accounts and unmanaged devices.

news.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
news.summary: CISA published a postmortem report on a data breach where a contractor accidentally exposed dozens of internal CISA credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, in a public GitHub repository for nearly six months. The incident highlights credential management failures and access control gaps, with security experts drawing lessons applicable to all security teams managing sensitive credentials.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two Scattered Spider members pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to the August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is a cybercrime group known for social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and phishing to bypass MFA and compromise accounts.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including those of the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal authentication and password reset controls were shared on Telegram.
